error_signature: Redis node only returns the last element of the list
title: Redis node limitation on list retrieval
symptom: The Redis node in n8n only retrieves the last element of a list instead of all elements.
applies_when: Trying to fetch all elements from a Redis list
not_this_if: Using a community Redis node that supports LRANGE
Using a Code node to execute custom Redis commands
root_cause: The built-in Redis node in n8n does not implement the LRANGE command, which is necessary for retrieving all elements from a list. As a result, users can only access the last element of the list or perform SET operations.
fix_steps: Set up a Redis community node that supports list commands like LRANGE.
Connect it to your existing Redis connection.
Use the command LRANGE your_list_key 0 -1 to retrieve all items.
Alternatively, attach a Code node that executes lrange('your:list:key', 0, -1) to fetch all list entries.
agent_instruction: Use a community Redis node or a Code node to retrieve all elements from the Redis list.
